expectations.text: Questions target specific gaps rather than restating the four stated requirements
expectations.evidence: Identifies 10 specific gaps ordered by rework risk. Questions target plugin contracts, UI extension meaning in CLI context, data source standardization, security, namespace collisions, state persistence, distribution, composability — all absent from user's statement. Notably, explored the codebase and discovered the existing plugin system, making questions very contextual.

expectations.text: Questions use concrete failure scenarios or edge cases rather than abstract inquiries
expectations.evidence: Q4 references specific codebase details: 'A third-party plugin can include arbitrary Python scripts... What's your plan for preventing a malicious or buggy plugin from reading your brain/ directory, exfiltrating API keys?' Q5 describes a concrete collision scenario: 'a research skill from core and a research skill from a third-party analytics plugin.' References actual files (justfile, marketplace.json).

expectations.text: Strategy explicitly plans to use AskUserQuestion one at a time
expectations.evidence: No mention of AskUserQuestion anywhere. Questions are presented as a numbered list without specifying the interaction mechanism.

expectations.text: At least one question surfaces a non-obvious architectural concern (beyond obvious security/sandboxing)
expectations.evidence: Q5 about skill trigger description collisions is a non-obvious concern specific to the existing NLP-based triggering architecture. Q8 about composability explicitly references the interview-me skill's own design pattern. Q6 about state persistence across sessions is subtle but important.

expectations.text: Strategy includes a mid-interview checkpoint or summary to redirect the conversation
expectations.evidence: No checkpoint or mid-interview summary is included. The 8 questions are presented as a linear sequence without any pause point to redirect based on accumulated answers.
